Vendor setup · Transcription

Set up Cohere

Cohere turns the physician's audio into text through its speech-to-text API — a high-quality transcription option for DirectScribe. Pick it in Settings and paste your Cohere key.

Transcription (STT) Confirm terms with Cohere

Role in DirectScribe
Transcription — converts your dictation to text.
Where your audio goes
To Cohere over an encrypted (TLS) connection, and nowhere else.
Privacy paperwork
Confirm whether Cohere will provide a Business Associate Agreement or equivalent data-processing terms for your use — check directly with Cohere.
Typical cost
Billed by Cohere per their current API pricing — confirm on Cohere's pricing page; see the cost breakdown for context.

Create your API key

  1. Create a Cohere account

    Go to dashboard.cohere.com and sign up.

  2. Open the API Keys page

    In the Cohere dashboard, open API Keys from the sidebar.

  3. Create a key

    Create a new API key (a production key for ongoing clinical use). Copy the key value and keep it somewhere safe.

  4. Paste it into DirectScribe

    In DirectScribe, open Settings → Providers → Transcription, choose Cohere, and paste the key. It is saved to your macOS Keychain.

Privacy paperwork

Confirm terms with Cohere

Before sending any patient-derived audio, confirm directly with Cohere what data-processing or Business Associate terms are available for your account, and whether they meet your practice's privacy assessment. Terms and pricing change — check the current details with Cohere.

Not legal advice — see the Compliance Centre. You decide, with your privacy advisor, whether a vendor's terms meet your obligations under PIPEDA and your provincial health-privacy law.
